sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 直接引语（包含三个并列句）
sentences.analysis: 该句为直接引语，包含三个并列的简单句，语气强烈，通过"watchdogs"和"lapdogs"的对比表达对最高法院裁决的批评。

sentences.en: The Supreme Court enters the final day of its session today after already giving Donald Trump several key victories and some setbacks.
sentences.cn: 最高法院今天进入其开庭期的最后一天，此前已经给了唐纳德·特朗普几次关键胜利和一些挫折。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语 + 谓语 + 宾语 + 时间状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为"The Supreme Court enters the final day of its session today"，后接"after"引导的时间状语从句，说明在此之前发生的事件。

sentences.en: Expected among those rulings, a key one on birthright citizenship, something the president tried to end with an executive order on his first day in office last year.
sentences.cn: 在这些裁决中，预计将有一项关于出生公民权的关键裁决，这是总统去年上任第一天试图通过行政命令废除的。
sentences.structure: 省略句：省略了"is"，完整为"A key one on birthright citizenship is expected among those rulings"，后接同位语从句
sentences.analysis: 句首"Expected among those rulings"为倒装结构，主语"a key one"后接"on birthright citizenship"作定语，"something"引导同位语从句解释说明。

sentences.en: Now, that is a fringe view in American law. Let's be clear. It's not a widely held view in the legal mainstream.
sentences.cn: 现在，这是美国法律中的一种边缘观点。我们要清楚。这不是法律主流中广泛持有的观点。
sentences.structure: 三个简单句并列
sentences.analysis: 通过三个短句强调该观点的非主流性，语气坚定。

sentences.en: You have heard him for months now, attacking justices on the Supreme Court by name, who he thinks are going to potentially rule against him on birthright citizenship; tearing them down over other rulings they've made that have been directly related to tariffs and other issues.
sentences.cn: 几个月来，你听到他点名攻击最高法院的法官，他认为这些法官可能会在出生公民权问题上对他不利；他还因他们做出的与关税和其他问题直接相关的其他裁决而诋毁他们。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 现在分词短语作伴随状语（包含定语从句）
sentences.analysis: 主句为"You have heard him"，后接两个现在分词短语"attacking..."和"tearing..."作伴随状语。第一个分词短语中包含"who"引导的定语从句修饰"justices"，第二个分词短语中包含"that"引导的定语从句修饰"rulings"。

sentences.en: But the way that he's been raising birthright citizenship for months in this context suggests that the administration knows, like you said, that this was a long shot.
sentences.cn: 但他几个月来在这种背景下提出出生公民权的方式表明，正如你所说，政府知道这希望渺茫。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 定语从句 + 宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句主语为"the way"，后接"that"引导的定语从句修饰"way"，谓语"suggests"后接"that"引导的宾语从句，其中包含插入语"like you said"。

sentences.en: And what that causes you to do is not spend any money talking to voters over a long period of time, because you're safe cycle after cycle.
sentences.cn: 而这导致你做的事情是，长期不花钱与选民沟通，因为你一个又一个周期都很安全。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主语从句 + 系表结构 + 原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: "What that causes you to do"为主语从句，系动词"is"后接不定式短语"not spend..."作表语，"because"引导原因状语从句。

sentences.en: And when you have these insurgent candidates where there's money being spent in a time where incumbents are looked at as poison, Democrat or Republican.
sentences.cn: 当你有了这些反叛的候选人，在现任者被视为毒药的时候，无论民主党还是共和党，都有资金投入。
sentences.structure: 复合句：时间状语从句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: "when"引导时间状语从句，其中包含"where"引导的定语从句修饰"candidates"，以及"where"引导的定语从句修饰"time"。

sentences.en: I think the thing that I wrestle with is that there are so many, both progressives and even, let's say, Congressional Black Caucus has this problem.
sentences.cn: 我认为我纠结的是，有这么多人，无论是进步派，甚至，比如说，国会黑人核心小组也有这个问题。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 宾语从句（包含定语从句和表语从句）
sentences.analysis: 主句为"I think"，后接"that"引导的宾语从句，其中主语"the thing"后接"that"引导的定语从句，系动词"is"后接"that"引导的表语从句。

sentences.en: And it's very tough when you're fighting against a wave.
sentences.cn: 当你与浪潮抗争时，这是非常艰难的。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 时间状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为"it's very tough"，"when"引导时间状语从句。

sentences.en: But it's also -- it's also important to note we're talking about blue district primaries in blue districts, in blue states.
sentences.cn: 但同样重要的是，我们谈论的是蓝色选区、蓝色州的蓝色选区初选。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为"it's important to note"，后接省略"that"的宾语从句。

sentences.en: It's one of those 40 seats that is literally 50/50.
sentences.cn: 这是那40个真正五五开的席位之一。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为"It's one of those 40 seats"，后接"that"引导的定语从句修饰"seats"。

sentences.en: This seat will determine who controls Congress and many seats just like that.
sentences.cn: 这个席位将决定谁控制国会以及许多类似的席位。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句 + 宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为"This seat will determine"，后接"who"引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: I'm a nobody. I'm just a young man who spent five days trapped beneath a building.
sentences.cn: 我是个无名小卒。我只是一个被困在建筑物下五天的年轻人。
sentences.structure: 两个简单句，第二句包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 第一句为简单句，第二句主语为"I"，后接"who"引导的定语从句修饰"young man"。

sentences.en: As far as I know, I was just informed that I was the only person in that building who was found alive.
sentences.cn: 据我所知，我刚刚被告知，我是那栋楼里唯一被发现活着的人。
sentences.structure: 复合句：状语从句 + 主句 + 宾语从句 + 定语从句
sentences.analysis: "As far as I know"为状语从句，主句为"I was just informed"，后接"that"引导的宾语从句，其中包含"who"引导的定语从句修饰"person"。

sentences.en: A CNN KFILE investigation uncovered deleted social media posts from Democratic congressional nominee Darializa Avila Chevalier's old Twitter account.
sentences.cn: CNN的KFILE调查发现了民主党国会候选人Darializa Avila Chevalier旧推特账户上已删除的社交媒体帖子。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构：主语(A CNN KFILE investigation) + 谓语(uncovered) + 宾语(deleted social media posts) + 介词短语(from...account)作定语修饰posts。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，主语是A CNN KFILE investigation，谓语是uncovered，宾语是deleted social media posts，from...account是介词短语作后置定语修饰posts。

sentences.en: And when asked about President Trump accusing her of being a communist, she said this.
sentences.cn: 当被问及特朗普总统指责她是共产主义者时，她这样说道。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句+主句：When asked about... (省略主语和be动词的状语从句) + 主句(she said this)。
sentences.analysis: when asked about...是过去分词短语作时间状语，相当于when she was asked about...，主句是she said this。

sentences.en: I think that is -- that framing is one that I've been very proud to be able to say I don't respond to.
sentences.cn: 我认为那种框架是我非常自豪能够说我不回应的。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+定语从句：I think (that) that framing is one (that I've been very proud to be able to say I don't respond to).
sentences.analysis: 主句是I think，后面是宾语从句，宾语从句中主语是that framing，系动词is，表语one，后面that引导定语从句修饰one，定语从句中I've been very proud to be able to say是谓语部分，say后面是省略that的宾语从句I don't respond to。

sentences.en: But I'll promise you, there is a Republican out there now taking those tweets and going to put them on an ad that says all Democrats are this way.
sentences.cn: 但我向你保证，现在有一个共和党人正在拿那些推文，准备把它们放在一则广告上，广告上说所有民主党人都是这样。
sentences.structure: 主句+宾语从句：I'll promise you (that) there is a Republican... + 定语从句(that says...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I'll promise you，后面是省略that的宾语从句，宾语从句是there be句型，主语a Republican，后面现在分词短语taking...和going to put...作定语修饰Republican，最后that引导定语从句修饰ad。

sentences.en: I think it's the biggest threat to our nation there is, maybe since our founding.
sentences.cn: 我认为这是自建国以来对我们国家最大的威胁。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句：I think (that) it's the biggest threat... + 定语从句(there is) + 状语(since our founding)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I think，后面是宾语从句，宾语从句中it's the biggest threat，后面there is是定语从句修饰threat，since our founding是时间状语。

sentences.en: And I just wonder why you can't just critique socialism as is, and you have to go straight to communism.
sentences.cn: 我只是想知道为什么你不能就事论事地批评社会主义，而必须直接跳到共产主义。
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：I wonder + 宾语从句(why you can't... and you have to...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I wonder，后面是why引导的宾语从句，从句中两个并列句由and连接。

sentences.en: And what we come down to in this is, in a time in a midterm, for all of you at home, national polls and the way the sentiment doesn't matter.
sentences.cn: 归根结底，在中期选举时期，对于所有在家观看的观众来说，全国民调和情绪走向并不重要。
sentences.structure: 主语从句+系表结构：What we come down to in this is + 表语从句(national polls... doesn't matter)。
sentences.analysis: 主语是what引导的主语从句What we come down to in this，系动词is，后面是表语从句，表语从句中主语是national polls and the way，谓语doesn't matter，the way后面省略了that/in which的定语从句the sentiment。

sentences.en: And the real basis comes down to, do you care more about somebody's regretful tweet or the price of hamburger and eggs?
sentences.cn: 真正的基础归结为：你更关心某人令人遗憾的推文，还是汉堡和鸡蛋的价格？
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：The real basis comes down to + 宾语从句(do you care more about... or...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是The real basis comes down to，后面是省略whether的宾语从句，从句是疑问句语序。

sentences.en: If you are advocating or advancing society in a direction that capital owners don't want, they're going to come after you.
sentences.cn: 如果你在倡导或推动社会朝着资本所有者不希望的方向发展，他们就会来找你。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句：If you are advocating... + 主句(they're going to come after you)。
sentences.analysis: if引导条件状语从句，从句中in a direction是介词短语作状语，后面that引导定语从句修饰direction。主句是they're going to come after you。

sentences.en: And if they can't find anything in your background, they're going to invent stuff, right?
sentences.cn: 如果他们找不到你背景中的任何东西，他们就会编造东西，对吧？
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句：If they can't find... + 主句(they're going to invent stuff)。
sentences.analysis: if引导条件状语从句，主句是they're going to invent stuff。

sentences.en: I think any state that privileges one religion over the other is one that I can't tell you I support, whether it be Israel or Saudi Arabia or anywhere else.
sentences.cn: 我认为任何优待一种宗教而非另一种宗教的国家，我都不能告诉你我支持它，无论是以色列、沙特阿拉伯还是其他任何地方。
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：I think (that) any state... is one... + 让步状语从句(whether it be...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I think，后面是宾语从句，宾语从句主语any state，后面that引导定语从句修饰state，系动词is，表语one，后面that引导定语从句修饰one，定语从句中I can't tell you I support，I support是省略that的宾语从句。最后whether...or...引导让步状语从句。

sentences.en: And a lot of that comes back to a fundamental belief that we should all be considered equal, no matter what our faith is.
sentences.cn: 其中很多都归结为一个基本信念：无论我们的信仰是什么，我们都应该被视为平等的。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+同位语从句：A lot of that comes back to a fundamental belief + 同位语从句(that we should...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是A lot of that comes back to a fundamental belief，后面that引导同位语从句解释belief，从句中no matter what引导让步状语从句。

sentences.en: And I also think, listen, it scares me, the anti-Semitic undertones that are happening throughout the country.
sentences.cn: 而且我还认为，听着，全国范围内出现的反犹太主义暗流让我感到害怕。
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：I think + 宾语从句(it scares me, the anti-Semitic undertones...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I think，后面是宾语从句，从句中it是形式主语，真正主语是the anti-Semitic undertones，后面that引导定语从句修饰undertones。

sentences.en: But I think that there is a way, though, of saying, pretty definitively, that after October 7th the conversation around Israel has changed tremendously.
sentences.cn: 但我认为，有一种方式可以相当明确地说，10月7日之后，围绕以色列的对话发生了巨大变化。
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：I think + 宾语从句(there is a way... of saying... that...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I think，后面是宾语从句，从句是there be句型，主语a way，后面of saying...是介词短语作定语，saying后面that引导宾语从句。

sentences.en: And then there's American's views on Israel in general, in 2022, 55 percent were favorable, in 2026, 37 percent.
sentences.cn: 然后是美国人总体上对以色列的看法，2022年55%的人持赞成态度，2026年37%。
sentences.structure: 并列句：There's American's views... + 两个并列分句(in 2022... in 2026...)。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句是there be句型，后面两个分句是并列的，省略了主语和谓语。

sentences.en: And I thought that was a weird, tacit acknowledgment by the White House that the public sentiment is not in Netanyahu's favor.
sentences.cn: 我认为这是白宫的一种奇怪的默示承认，即公众情绪不利于内塔尼亚胡。
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：I thought + 宾语从句(that was a weird... acknowledgment... that...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I thought，后面是宾语从句，从句主语that，系动词was，表语acknowledgment，后面that引导同位语从句解释acknowledgment。

sentences.en: But I do want to step back and -- we do this thing of dissecting our politics as if all the things are not related.
sentences.cn: 但我确实想退一步——我们做这种剖析政治的事情，好像所有事情都不相关。
sentences.structure: 并列句：I do want to step back + 并列分句(we do this thing... as if...)。
sentences.analysis: 第一个分句是I do want to step back，第二个分句是we do this thing，后面as if引导方式状语从句。

sentences.en: Every dollar we send abroad to drop a bomb on another kid is a dollar we don't spend here to invest in your kids' school or your health care.
sentences.cn: 我们寄往国外投在另一个孩子身上的每一美元，都是我们没有花在这里投资于你孩子的学校或你的医疗保健的一美元。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构：Every dollar (we send abroad...) is a dollar (we don't spend...)。
sentences.analysis: 主语Every dollar，后面省略that的定语从句we send abroad to drop a bomb on another kid，系动词is，表语a dollar，后面省略that的定语从句we don't spend here to invest...。

sentences.en: And the politicians, the politics, that speaks to the lived experience of the average person that actually talks about how do we put money back in your pocket?
sentences.cn: 而那些政治家、政治，那些与普通人的生活经历对话、真正谈论如何把钱放回你口袋的政治？
sentences.structure: 主语+定语从句：The politicians, the politics, + 定语从句(that speaks to... that actually talks about...)。
sentences.analysis: 主语是the politicians, the politics，后面that引导定语从句，从句中又包含一个that引导的定语从句修饰the lived experience，第二个定语从句中how do we put money back in your pocket是宾语从句。

sentences.en: But so long as you're having this nagging debate about where the party is going from your comfortable home in wherever it is, I just think you're missing the point because too many people are uncomfortable in their homes or don't have a home in the first place.
sentences.cn: 但只要你从你舒适的家中（无论在哪里）进行这场关于党走向何方的令人厌烦的辩论，我就认为你忽略了重点，因为太多人在自己的家中感到不舒服，或者根本没有家。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句：So long as you're having... + 主句(I just think...)。
sentences.analysis: so long as引导条件状语从句，从句中about where the party is going是介词短语作定语，from your comfortable home是状语，in wherever it is是地点状语从句。主句是I just think，后面是宾语从句，宾语从句中because引导原因状语从句。

sentences.en: And to bring somebody in to represent your campaign, in my mind, is a very different decision than going on somebody's stream and talking to their audience.
sentences.cn: 在我看来，请某人来代表你的竞选，与去某人的直播中与他们的观众交谈，是一个非常不同的决定。
sentences.structure: 主语+系表结构：To bring somebody in... is a very different decision than...。
sentences.analysis: 主语是不定式短语To bring somebody in to represent your campaign，系动词is，表语a very different decision，后面than引导比较状语，比较对象是going on somebody's stream and talking to their audience。

sentences.en: And I think we have to take seriously the idea that the money that we spent to kill other people's kids there was money that we did not spend to take care of the kids that I was taking care of in Wayne County in Detroit to make sure they had --
sentences.cn: 而且我认为我们必须认真对待这样一个想法：我们花在杀死别人孩子上的钱，是我们没有花在照顾我在底特律韦恩县照顾的孩子上的钱，以确保他们拥有——
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：I think + 宾语从句(we have to take seriously the idea that...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I think，后面是宾语从句，从句中take seriously是动词短语，宾语the idea，后面that引导同位语从句，同位语从句中主语the money，后面that引导定语从句修饰money，系动词was，表语money，后面that引导定语从句修饰money，定语从句中又包含that引导的定语从句修饰the kids。

sentences.en: The idea that we're going to tie people together and say, well, guilt by association so is just not in keeping with the way most of us think about the world.
sentences.cn: 那种我们要把人们联系在一起并说“好吧，关联有罪”的想法，根本不符合我们大多数人看待世界的方式。
sentences.structure: 主语+系表结构：The idea (that we're going to tie... and say...) is just not in keeping with the way...。
sentences.analysis: 主语The idea，后面that引导同位语从句，从句中两个并列谓语tie和say，系动词is，表语not in keeping with the way，后面省略that/in which的定语从句most of us think about the world。

sentences.en: And whether we're talking about the parties writ large or we're talking about our Michigan race, it's on people to get to decide.
sentences.cn: 无论我们是在谈论整个政党，还是在谈论我们密歇根州的竞选，决定权都在人民手中。
sentences.structure: 让步状语从句+主句：Whether we're talking about... or... + 主句(it's on people to decide)。
sentences.analysis: whether...or...引导让步状语从句，主句是it's on people to get to decide，it是形式主语，真正主语是不定式短语to get to decide。

sentences.en: And this is just what I'm saying.
sentences.cn: 这正是我所说的。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构：This is + 表语从句(what I'm saying)。
sentences.analysis: 主语This，系动词is，后面what引导表语从句。

sentences.en: So long as we are missing the conversation about everyday people and why they are struggling in their lives, we are missing the point of Democratic politics in the first place.
sentences.cn: 只要我们错过了关于普通民众以及他们为何在生活中挣扎的对话，我们就首先错过了民主政治的意义。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句：So long as we are missing... + 主句(we are missing...)。
sentences.analysis: so long as引导条件状语从句，从句中about everyday people and why they are struggling是介词短语作定语，why引导宾语从句。主句是we are missing the point。

sentences.en: And they keep telling me that I'm not going to be able to get further.
sentences.cn: 他们一直告诉我，我无法走得更远。
sentences.structure: 主谓+宾语从句：They keep telling me + 宾语从句(that I'm not going to be able to get further)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是They keep telling me，后面that引导宾语从句。

sentences.en: But I'll also tell you this, I'm not going to pretend like not calling out the problems that I see is going to make things better.
sentences.cn: 但我也会告诉你这一点：我不会假装不指出我看到的问题会让事情变得更好。
sentences.structure: 主谓+双宾语+同位语从句：I'll tell you this + 同位语从句(I'm not going to pretend...)。
sentences.analysis: 主句是I'll tell you this，this指代后面整个句子，后面句子是同位语从句，从句中pretend like后面是宾语从句，宾语从句主语是not calling out the problems，problems后面that引导定语从句，系动词is，表语going to make things better。

sentences.en: And the real conversation is not between me and 99 other senators when I get there, it's between me and the 350 million people.
sentences.cn: 真正的对话不是在我和另外99名参议员之间（当我到那里时），而是在我和3.5亿人民之间。
sentences.structure: 并列句：The real conversation is not between... when..., it's between...。
sentences.analysis: 两个并列分句，第一个分句中包含when引导的时间状语从句。

sentences.en: And that's the mistake that I think both parties are making.
sentences.cn: 这就是我认为两党都在犯的错误。
sentences.structure: 主系表+定语从句：That's the mistake + 定语从句(that I think both parties are making)。
sentences.analysis: 主语That，系动词is，表语the mistake，后面that引导定语从句，从句中I think是插入语，both parties are making是宾语从句。

topics.title: 美国最高法院裁决
topics.content: 美国最高法院在2026年6月30日发布了一系列裁决，涉及总统权力、出生公民权、独立机构官员任免等议题。裁决结果好坏参半，总统特朗普获得了一些关键胜利，但也遭遇了挫折。其中，关于出生公民权的裁决备受关注，特朗普曾试图通过行政命令废除该权利，但最高法院可能不会支持。此外，法院还裁定总统可以随意解雇独立机构官员，但联邦储备委员会因其特殊性而例外。
topics.keywords: Supreme Court, birthright citizenship, executive power, independent agencies, Federal Reserve

topics.title: 科罗拉多州初选与民主党内部代际冲突
topics.content: 科罗拉多州举行初选，29岁的民主社会主义者Melat Kiros挑战已任职30年的现任议员Diana Degette。这反映了民主党内部建制派与进步派之间的代际冲突，以及选民对长期执政的现任者的不满。类似情况也在纽约、华盛顿特区等地出现，民主社会主义者正在崛起。
topics.keywords: Colorado primary, Democratic socialist, incumbent, generational shift, progressive

topics.title: 委内瑞拉地震救援
topics.content: 委内瑞拉发生地震，造成约1700人死亡，5000人受伤，多人失踪。救援人员从废墟中救出幸存者，包括一名12岁儿童和一名被困5天的21岁男子。还有宠物被救出。

topics.title: 前NFL球星克里斯·约翰逊患渐冻症
topics.content: 前NFL跑卫克里斯·约翰逊（Chris Johnson）在电视采访中透露自己患有肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS），只能通过语音生成设备交流。他是NFL历史上仅有的9个单赛季冲球超过2000码的球员之一。
topics.keywords: Chris Johnson, NFL, ALS, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, speech generation device

topics.title: 世界杯爆冷：巴拉圭淘汰德国
topics.content: 在世界杯比赛中，巴拉圭通过点球大战击败四届冠军德国队，晋级16强。这是德国队首次在世界杯点球大战中失利。
topics.keywords: World Cup, Paraguay, Germany, upset, penalty shootout

topics.title: 美国民主党内部意识形态分歧
topics.content: 本文讨论了民主党内部因候选人过往社交媒体言论（如支持共产主义、马克思主义）引发的争议，以及党内进步派与温和派在以色列政策、经济议题上的分歧。部分评论将当前进步派崛起与共和党2010年茶党运动类比，认为可能引发党内分裂。
topics.keywords: Democratic Party, progressive, moderate, schism, socialism, communism, Tea Party

topics.title: 美国对以色列政策与公众舆论变化
topics.content: 节目分析了美国民众对以色列看法的转变，特别是年轻民主党人中对以色列持负面看法的比例高达84%。同时讨论了国会内部关于对以色列军事援助的争议，以及进步派要求改变政策的压力。还提及特朗普政府对内塔尼亚胡的不满。
topics.keywords: Israel, Gaza, military aid, public opinion, Democratic Party, Netanyahu, AIPAC

topics.title: 社交媒体言论对政治候选人的影响
topics.content: 候选人过去在社交媒体上的言论成为竞选攻击的焦点。节目探讨了“挖掘旧推文”作为政治手段的公平性，以及选民是否更关心经济问题而非候选人的历史言论。同时提到“阅后即焚”应用因避免留下痕迹而日益流行。
topics.keywords: social media, deleted posts, opposition research, vetting, fair game, disappearing apps

topics.title: 2026年中期选举与关键议题
topics.content: 讨论聚焦于中期选举中哪些议题真正影响选民，如经济、物价、工资等。战略分析师认为全国民调对具体选区影响有限，关键在郊区选民的关注点。同时提及密歇根州参议员初选，候选人围绕是否接受企业捐款、AI监管等议题展开辩论。
topics.keywords: midterm election, swing voters, economy, inflation, Michigan Senate race, corporate money

topics.title: 人工智能监管与民主控制
topics.content: 候选人Abdul El-Sayed主张对AI技术进行民主监督，认为应防止其被少数公司控制。他拒绝接受企业捐款，包括AI公司，并呼吁其他民主党人也这样做。这反映了进步派对科技巨头影响力的担忧。
topics.keywords: artificial intelligence, democratic oversight, corporate money, regulation, technology
